rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Good afternoon, sir.
Where is the bus stand in this town ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: There is a police station at the end of this road.
Behind it is the bus-stand.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Is there a railway station in this town ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, it is also beside the bus stand.
Are you new to this place ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Yes, I am new to this place.

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Which place do you belong to ?

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: I belong to Dharwar.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Is there a bus to Hampi now ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: There are plenty.
There is a bus every half an hour.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: At what time is the next bus ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: There are two buses at one thirty.
There is a bus in the third platform.
Another one is in the fourth.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Are there seats available in this bus ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, there are plenty.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Is there a dog in yonr house ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: No. There is no dog in our house.
There is one in our farm.

rows.speaker: A
rows.english: Oh ! Do you own a farm ?

rows.speaker: B
rows.english: Yes, don't you have one ?
